step.title: Build Configuration
step.goal: Write skill.yaml — the vera-cli artifact descriptor. Configure type, id, destination, file_name, includes, and attachments.

substep.title: Compose skill.yaml
substep.action:
Write .vera/skills/{{skill_id}}/skill.yaml with the dubstack-native artifact descriptor shape:
Workflow skill: ```yaml type: skill id: {{skill_id}} destination: {{skill_id}} file_name: SKILL.md includes: - head.md - shared/workflow-engine.md - skill-overview.md attachments: - references/ ```
Exec skill: ```yaml type: skill id: {{skill_id}} destination: {{skill_id}} file_name: SKILL.md includes: - head.md - skill-overview.md attachments: - references/ # only if references/ has scripts or other content ```
Data skill (flat layout): ```yaml type: skill id: {{skill_id}} destination: {{skill_id}} file_name: SKILL.md includes: - SKILL.md ```
Additional attachments for skills that consume shared resources: - shared/scripts/ # cross-skill scripts - shared/references/{namespace}/ # cross-skill references

substep.title: Confirm Build Config
substep.template-output:
Present the written skill.yaml for review:
Key checks: - type: skill - id matches directory name - file_name is SKILL.md - includes order matters (head.md first, then engine for workflows, then overview last) - attachments list resource directories that get copied to runtime
[a] Adjust [c] Continue [y] YOLO

rules.rule: includes: concatenated INTO the compiled SKILL.md body in order
attachments: copied as resource files alongside SKILL.md at runtime
head.md is always first include (provides --- name/description --- frontmatter)
For workflow skills, shared/workflow-engine.md comes second (engine before body)
skill-overview.md is always last include (provides the <skill> XML body)
shared/scripts/ — attach when the skill invokes any shared script
shared/references/{namespace}/ — attach when the skill consumes any cross-skill reference
Skill-local references stay at references/ (no shared/ prefix)

section.title: destination Field
section.description: destination controls where the compiled artifact is written under the target's directory. Conventionally matches the skill id, but can differ when multiple skills should compile to the same directory or when a custom layout is needed.
